source_jobs.title: Software Engineer ERP Integrations
source_jobs.description:
Join BLP Digital — The #1 Solution for ERP Automation

BLP Digital is redefining ERP automation with agentic AI. Spun out of ETH Zurich and HSG, we build AI agents that automate finance, procurement, logistics, sales, and more for some of the world's largest enterprises. We solve real enterprise problems with cutting-edge technology and a strong sense of ownership.

Our solution is live in 40+ countries, used by 20,000+ daily active users, and automates 70,000+ processes every day, including for Fortune 500 companies. As one of Switzerland's fastest-growing SaaS scaleups, our success stems from deep expertise in technology and business processes, delivering a product with outstanding product-market fit, proven by a growing global customer base.

We've recently welcomed Goldman Sachs Alternatives as a growth investor, and we're now expanding into the UK with the launch of our London office. We are just getting started. Ready to build the future? Join BLP Digital today.


Location: This is a full-time position and can either be filled on-site at our office in central Zurich, Switzerland or remotely from Germany - This is a hybrid role (3-4 days in the office, 1-2 days working from home).

Job Summary

As Integrations Engineer, you will play a crucial role in enabling our customers to connect their ERP systems with BLP Digital’s platform. This involves designing and implementing interfaces, handling a variety of protocols such as REST, SOAP, and direct database access, and managing file transfers. Additionally, you will map incoming EDI and e-invoice formats to align with our standards. Working closely with our Project Team and client stakeholders, your role will be essential to the technical integration of new clients, contributing directly to our business growth. Proficiency in German is a requirement, as the majority of our customers are based in the DACH region.

Main Duties: 

 - Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to enable seamless ERP integration.

 - Work on our standard API’s using Go and Java, including further development and task execution.

 - Design and implement interfaces for diverse protocols (REST, SOAP, direct DB access, file transfers).

 - Translate and map incoming EDI and e-invoice formats to our standards.

 - Explore new ERP systems and determine optimal communication methods.

 - Document new interfaces and features.

 - Work with existing customers and maintain their interfaces.



Requirements: 

 - Bachelor's degree or higher in computer science or a related field (or equivalent experience).

 - 3 years of experience in technical role, ideally linked to integrations and/or ERP systems

 - Proficiency in English, on top proficiency in German is preferred.

 - Experience with Go, Java, REST/SOAP, and SQL.

 - Ideally, professional experience in a client-facing role.

 - Structured and self-reliant working approach.

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.



Benefits: 

 - Generate real impact and lasting value with your work.

 - Opportunity to work at the forefront of ERP process automation innovation.

 - Fast-tracked promotions and an attractive bonus scheme, including company shares.

 - A collaborative environment that values teamwork and excellence.

 - Exposure to state-of-the-art technologies and continuous learning opportunities.

 - Choose your own hardware and join regular team events.

 - If the role is filled remotely from Germany: Paid visits and stay in Zurich to get to know the team in person.

source_jobs.title: ERP Integration Architect
source_jobs.description:
Join BLP Digital — The #1 Solution for ERP Automation

BLP Digital is redefining ERP automation with agentic AI. Spun out of ETH Zurich and HSG, we build AI agents that automate finance, procurement, logistics, sales, and more for some of the world's largest enterprises. We solve real enterprise problems with cutting-edge technology and a strong sense of ownership.

Our solution is live in 40+ countries, used by 20,000+ daily active users, and automates 70,000+ processes every day, including for Fortune 500 companies. As one of Switzerland's fastest-growing SaaS scaleups, our success stems from deep expertise in technology and business processes, delivering a product with outstanding product-market fit, proven by a growing global customer base.

We've recently welcomed Goldman Sachs Alternatives as a growth investor, and we're now expanding into the UK with the launch of our London office. We are just getting started. Ready to build the future? Join BLP Digital today.

About the Job

The future of enterprise software is clear: traditional applications will remain systems of record, storing the truth of business data. But the real value will come from AI-driven systems of action — software that understands, decides, and executes.

At blp, this future is already here. Our SaaS digital twin ERP enables AI to understand, decide and execute ERP processes. To make this possible, our Integrations Team builds the bridges between customer ERPs and blp — turning fragmented systems of record into a unified system of action.

As an Integrations Architect, you’ll:

 - Dive into new processes and ERP systems, mastering their complexity

 - Collaborate directly with customers to design and deliver seamless integrations

 - Own existing and new integrations end-to-end, shaping how global enterprises connect to blp

This is NOT a narrow coding role. It’s about building the foundation for how AI transforms enterprise technology. You’ll collaborate directly with customers, own existing and new integrations, and make sure blp becomes the most seamless ERP automation layer in the market. Combine technical expertise, process understanding, and customer interaction to drive impact at scale.

Join us and help move enterprises from systems of record to systems of action. And in doing so, help build the future of AI technology.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Architect and own integration projects connecting blp to our client ERPs (today we integrate 30+ ERPs, tomorrow we aim for 300+)

 - Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand integration requirements and translate them into technical specifications and deliverables.

 - Define integration standards and ensure new implementations consistently meet them

 - Standardize, document, and continuously improve integration workflows

 - Create playbooks, templates, and self-service resources for partners and customers



Requirements - Must:

 - 3+ years of experience in ERP integration, ERP development, or technical ERP consulting

 - Solid understanding of ERP processes & systems (SAP, Microsoft Dynamics, proAlpha, abas, or others)

 - Experience in software development including cloud infrastructure, strong knowledge of Git, CI/CD pipelines and modern dev workflows

 - Advanced knowledge of relational databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL)

 - Strong knowledge of APIs, middleware, and SaaS integrations

 - Ability to work directly with customers and translate business requirements into technical solutions

 - Communication skills in German and English



Benefits:

 - The job is anything but 0-8-15, every day the same thing. You’ll learn about 20+ ERP systems, different processes, and become an expert in enterprise integrations

 - Lead and own projects end-to-end with full accountability

 - Collaborate directly with global industry leaders and their IT teams

 - Remote flexibility: Work from anywhere, with HQ space in Zürich if you prefer

 - Shape a fast-scaling AI company from the ground up

 - Compe

 - Fair lead allocation. We make sure the playing field is level for everyone. AI algorithms decide, not managers

 - High Earning Potential. Uncapped commission and expected 6-figure package, and opportunity to participate in common share equity program

 - Equal OTEs. As we share the same incentives, you’ll be comped as the Account Executives

source_jobs.description:

Our solution is live in 40+ countries, used by 20,000+ daily active users, and automates 70,000+ processes every day, including for Fortune 500 companies. As one of Switzerland's fastest-growing SaaS scaleups, our success stems from deep expertise in technology and business processes, delivering a product with outstanding product-market fit, proven by a growing global customer base.

We've recently welcomed Goldman Sachs Alternatives as a growth investor, and we're now expanding into the UK with the launch of our London office. We are just getting started. Ready to build the future? Join BLP Digital today.

How We Work

 - AI-First & Data-Driven: We leverage the latest tech (or build our own) so people focus on the work that matters most

 - In control: As a self-financed scale-up, we make decisions—not investors

 - Ownership: We own our work, our wins, and our mistakes. It's how we grow

 - Excellence: We don't settle for "good enough." Exceptional is the bar

 - Transparency: Open communication, honest processes, no surprises

 - Candour: Bold, direct conversations that unlock better ideas and outcomes

About the Role

We're hiring a Strategic Account Executive to win and grow strategic enterprise accounts. You will own the commercial outcome—from pipeline creation to close—by practicing insight-led selling: reframing the customer's initial problem, quantifying the cost of inaction, and mobilizing buying committees around a compelling "why now."

You'll lead multi-stakeholder enterprise cycles with CFO/CIO/Shared Services/Procurement leadership, partnering closely with Solutions Engineering, Marketing, and Customer Success to run disciplined deal plans, deliver value-based proposals, and land enterprise rollouts.

What You'll Own

Pipeline creation & territory strategy

 - Build and execute a territory/account plan: target accounts, stakeholders, sequencing, and multi-threading strategy.

 - Create net-new pipeline through outbound prospecting, partner motion, events, and customer referrals.

 - Maintain disciplined pipeline hygiene and forecasting; operate a repeatable, metrics-driven cadence.

Insight-led selling & deal shaping

 - Practice insight selling: challenge the customer's initial framing and introduce a sharper point of view on where value is trapped (exceptions, approvals, master data, controls).

 - Run executive discovery to uncover operational bottlenecks and decision friction—and translate it into a crisp target-state narrative, urgency, and mutual action plan.

 - Build internal alignment across CFO/CIO-led buying committees, including champions, economic buyers, IT/security, and process owners.

Commercial ownership: value case, negotiation & close

 - Own the business case, pricing strategy, proposals, negotiation, and contracting.

 - Quantify and communicate value (capacity release, cycle time reduction, control/compliance improvements, stack simplification) and tie it to executive priorities and budget.

 - Drive the deal to closure with clear next steps, strong deal governance, and accurate forecasting.

Orchestration across presales & delivery

 - Partner with Solutions Engineers to run workshops/demos and de-risk technical concerns (security, integration, rollout).

 - Align with Customer Success on implementation readiness, success criteria, and expansion paths.

 - Ensure clean handoffs from close → kickoff, maintaining continuity of value narrative and outcomes.

Competitive positioning

 - Position blp against point tools and fragmented stacks (OCR/IDP/workflow/process mining/RPA) with an "end-to-end + exceptions-first" narrative.

· Ownership: We own our work, our wins, and our mistakes. It’s how we grow and improve

· Transparency: What you see is what you get. Open communication, honest processes, and no surprises
· Candour: Bold, honest conversations that inspire new ideas and solve problems

About the role

We’re hiring an Enterprise Solutions Engineer (Presales) to shape and win enterprise ERP automation decisions: you reframe problems, quantify the cost of inaction, and prove value through tailored workshops and demos. Partnering with an AE, you own technical leadership, value articulation, and de-risking security/integration—building alignment across CFO/CIO-led buying committees.

What You’ll Own


Insight-led discovery & deal shaping

 - Practice insight selling: challenge the customer’s initial framing, introduce a sharper point of view on where value is actually trapped (exceptions, approvals, master data, controls), and reframe urgency using cost-of-inaction.

 - Lead discovery across CFO/CIO/Shared Services/Procurement to uncover operational bottlenecks and decision friction—and translate that into a crisp target-state narrative and mutual action plan.

Solution design & technical leadership

 - Architect a tailored solution: scope, rollout approach, integration pattern, security posture, and success criteria.

 - Run deep dives with IT/security and articulate how blp works in enterprise environments (ERP digital twin, data synchronization, governance and controls).

Demos, workshops & executive storytelling

 - Deliver high-impact demos that map to customer workflows (P2P, AP, S2P) and prove outcomes—not features.

 - Facilitate workshops that turn stakeholder skepticism into a tangible blueprint and path to value.

Value engineering (business case support)

 - Build a credible value narrative: labor productivity capacity, cycle time reduction, compliance/control improvements, and stack simplification.

 - Support champions and the AE with executive-ready business-case inputs, impact ranges, and rollout economics.

Competitive positioning

 - Position blp against point tools and fragmented stacks (OCR/IDP/workflow/process mining/RPA) with an “end-to-end + exceptions-first” narrative.

 - Help the deal team land a clear “why now” anchored in operational risk, ERP program success, and enterprise scale requirements.

What We’re Looking For
Must-haves

 - 3–8+ years in Solutions Engineering / Presales / Solution Consulting / ERP process consulting (or equivalent in finance/procurement operations with strong customer-facing chops).

 - Demonstrated ability to run insight-based discovery (Challenger-style): you can respectfully push back, quantify impact,

We're looking for a Team Lead to head our Platform SAT Team — a group of 5 senior engineers (growing to 8) who serve as the connective tissue across our biggest technical initiatives. Each engineer on the team owns a major cross-cutting project, working with and through other teams to deliver platform-level outcomes. Your job is to lead them, grow the team, and stay deeply hands-on with the code.

This is not a pure people-management role. You'll review PRs, jump on production incidents, profile slow MySQL queries, and ship features alongside your team. You'll also do the harder leadership work: setting technical direction, removing blockers across team boundaries, and helping grow the team to 8.

We work in Go. We don't require prior Go experience — strong engineers learn it quickly — but you should be ready to ramp up fast and be writing production Go on the team from day one.

 

What You'll Do

Lead and mentor a team of 5 senior engineers, each working on a different high-impact platform project

 1. Help grow the team to 8 — interviewing, calibrating, and closing candidates

 2. Coordinate across engineering teams to align priorities, unblock dependencies, and drive cross-team initiatives to completion

 3. Write and review Go code daily; set the bar for code quality, correctness, and design

 4. Optimize MySQL performance — query tuning, indexing strategy, schema design, and scaling decisions

 5. Participate in the on-call rotation and lead incident response when production issues hit

 6. Establish and enforce strong engineering principles: correctness, observability, documentation, code review standards, and testing discipline

 7. Run 1:1s, performance reviews, career development conversations, and team rituals

 8. Translate business priorities into a technical roadmap your team can execute onead and mentor a team of 5 senior engineers, each working on a different high-impact platform project



What You Bring

Required

 1. Proven management experience leading a team of senior engineers (5+), including hiring, performance management, and career development

 2. Deep MySQL knowledge — query optimization, indexing, replication, and performance tuning under real load

 3. Experience operating production systems: on-call, incident response, observability, postmortems

 4. Experience with functional programming concepts and languages. Your understanding of software quality goes beyond aesthetics and best practices

 5. Your decisions are not limited by knowledge: you are comfortable with continuous studying to ensure you make the best choices

 6. You are able to think outside of specific languages and frameworks

 7. Track record of establishing engineering principles and raising the bar on code quality, correctness, and testing

 8. Comfort working across team boundaries — you can build alignment, navigate competing priorities, and get things done without formal authority over other teams

 9. Excellent written and verbal communication

 - Ownership: We own our work, our wins, and our mistakes. It's how we grow

 - Excellence: We don't settle for "good enough." Exceptional is the bar

 - Transparency: Open communication, honest processes, no surprises

 - Candour: Bold, direct conversations that unlock better ideas and outcomes

About the Role

We're hiring a Senior Strategic Account Executive to win and grow strategic enterprise accounts. You will own the commercial outcome—from pipeline creation to close—by practicing insight-led selling: reframing the customer's initial problem, quantifying the cost of inaction, and mobilizing buying committees around a compelling "why now."

You'll lead multi-stakeholder enterprise cycles with CFO/CIO/Shared Services/Procurement leadership, partnering closely with Solutions Engineering, Marketing, and Customer Success to run disciplined deal plans, deliver value-based proposals, and land enterprise rollouts.

What You'll Own

Pipeline creation & territory strategy

 - Build and execute a territory/account plan: target accounts, stakeholders, sequencing, and multi-threading strategy.

 - Create net-new pipeline through outbound prospecting, partner motion, events, and customer referrals.

 - Maintain disciplined pipeline hygiene and forecasting; operate a repeatable, metrics-driven cadence.

Insight-led selling & deal shaping

 - Practice insight selling: challenge the customer's initial framing and introduce a sharper point of view on where value is trapped (exceptions, approvals, master data, controls).

 - Run executive discovery to uncover operational bottlenecks and decision friction—and translate it into a crisp target-state narrative, urgency, and mutual action plan.

 - Build internal alignment across CFO/CIO-led buying committees, including champions, economic buyers, IT/security, and process owners.

Commercial ownership: value case, negotiation & close

 - Own the business case, pricing strategy, proposals, negotiation, and contracting.

 - Quantify and communicate value (capacity release, cycle time reduction, control/compliance improvements, stack simplification) and tie it to executive priorities and budget.

 - Drive the deal to closure with clear next steps, strong deal governance, and accurate forecasting.

Orchestration across presales & delivery

 - Partner with Solutions Engineers to run workshops/demos and de-risk technical concerns (security, integration, rollout).

 - Align with Customer Success on implementation readiness, success criteria, and expansion paths.

 - Ensure clean handoffs from close → kickoff, maintaining continuity of value narrative and outcomes.

Competitive positioning

 - Position BLP against point tools and fragmented stacks (OCR/IDP/workflow/process mining/RPA) with an "end-to-end + exceptions-first" narra

We've recently welcomed Goldman Sachs Alternatives as a growth investor, and we're now expanding into the UK with the launch of our London office. We are just getting started. Ready to build the future? Join BLP Digital today.

About the position

We're looking for the top 1% - engineers who treat their craft like athletes treat performance, and who want to work alongside people who do the same. The world has changed dramatically in the last quarter. What used to take a team of consultants six months now takes a sharp engineer a few days. The momentum is unreal, and we're growing into it fast.

As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you're embedded directly in our customers' environments, taking a messy enterprise problem from first conversation to a working production system in days - not quarters. You work as part of a small senior team - alongside a Senior Consultant and other FDEs - and you own the technical solution end-to-end: architecture, integrations, agent and workflow logic, frontend where needed, evals, deployment. The work starts and ends with the customer, not the codebase. Feedback cycles are measured in hours, the bar is high, and the people you ship with will push you to raise it.


Key Responsibilities

 - Sit with end-users, process owners, and client engineers to understand the real workflow - and the real problem underneath the stated one

 - Translate the target-state process into a concrete technical solution: architecture, data model, integrations, agent and workflow design - and ship it in days and weeks, not quarters

 - Build full-stack: TypeScript backend, React frontend where needed, integrations, LLM orchestration, evals

 - Demo early and often. Put working software in front of real users and fold their feedback into the next iteration

 - Make speed / quality / cost trade-offs in real time. Choose build vs. buy vs. configure existing platform capability

 - Own observability and evals once live. Iterate until the system is reliable in the customer's real conditions

 - Earn trust with the client's own engineering and security teams - API access, data flows, deployment, reviews

 - Bring patterns back - reusable components, prompt structures, integration approaches - so the next engagement ships faster

Requirements

 - Builder background with real shipping experience - you've taken systems from zero to production and kept them running

 - Fluent with modern AI tooling: LLM APIs, tool and function calling, agent frameworks, evals, prompt engineering as a real discipline

 - Strong with TypeScript on the backend and React (or equivalent) on the frontend. Comfortable with APIs, auth, queues, and integrating with legacy systems

 - Daily, fluent use of Claude Code, Cursor, or similar - expected to ship meaningfully faster than a traditional engineer because of them

 - Customer-facing instinct - you genuinely enjoy being in the room with users, asking sharp questions, and pushing back respectfully when needed

 - You move fast, communicate clearly, and operate well with ambiguity. Comfortable being dropped into an unfamiliar domain and coming back with a working prototype

 - Fluency in English. German is a plus

 - Bonus: experience automating business processes i
